schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: # 2 — Objects & notation - Let the vacuum be a continuum (or very large network) of phase oscillators indexed by \(x\) (a spatial position) or discrete index \(i\). - Each oscillator has phase \(\varphi_i(t)\) and base (natural) frequency \(\nu_0\) (rad/s: \(\omega_0\)). - Coupling kernel between oscillators: \(K_{ij}\) (or \(K(x,x')\) continuum). - A persistent phase offset / half-step perturbation we denote \(\Delta\varphi_{\rm drop}\) (you described as a “half-step out of beat”—we model that as a sudden or slowly varying phase shift of magnitude \(\approx\pi\) or \(\pi/2\); later we keep it a parameter). - Local creation (birth) events of quasi-particles occur at rate \(\lambda_i(t)\) that depends on local phase dynamics (size of phase slip, local coherence). - Each created quasi-particle deposits an effective “mass packet” \(m_0\) (very small) and has a tiny transfer momentum \(p_0\). Accumulation of \(N\) packets → mass density \(\rho\sim N m_0\). - Emergent gravitational acceleration at point \(x\) is generated by net flux of quasi-particles toward denser regions — modeled with a Poisson-like equation (source term = \(\rho\) built from creation events).
theory.technical_interpretation: Objects & Notation: p_0 is a symbolic expression, written p_0. It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from p_0. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where each created quasi-particle deposits an effective “mass packet” (very small) and has a tiny transfer momentum
